adjustments: Nico Schlotterbeck ruled out, prompting discussion of how Germany may need to adjust their build-up structure
Antonio Rüdiger is identified as the likely direct replacement, though he does not offer the same ball-playing profile
phases.summary: This phase is not about live in-game tactical developments so much as an update with implications for Germany’s structure. Schlotterbeck’s absence removes an important build-up defender, forcing Julian Nagelsmann to rethink how Germany progress the ball from the back. Rüdiger is expected to step in, but the commentary stresses that his profile is different and less suited to the same build-up role.
